title: CONTRIBUTING.md and club adaptation guide
description: Create a CONTRIBUTING.md that covers two things: (1) standard contribution guidelines for developers who want to submit code, and (2) a practical 'Club Adaptation Guide' section for technically-minded people at other clubs who want to deploy their own instance. The adaptation guide should be honest about what's involved — this was built for St Ann's and there are hardcoded assumptions that would need changing — without being discouraging. It should give a clear picture of the effort required so people can make an informed decision.
acceptance_criteria: Developer contribution section covers: how to set up the development environment, how to run tests, code style expectations, PR process
Club Adaptation Guide section exists with a clear, honest overview of what's involved
Guide documents the known St Ann's-specific assumptions: ClubID-based home/away logic, hardcoded club references, apostrophe handling in 'St Ann's' across various data sources
Guide lists the key files and configuration that would need changing for a different club
Guide provides a rough categorisation of effort: what's straightforward config, what needs code changes, what's a known pain point
Guide explicitly acknowledges the apostrophe problem as known tech debt without attempting to solve it in this sprint
Guide points to the GitHub issue template for clubs that want help or have questions
Tone is welcoming but realistic — 'here's what you'd need to do' not 'it's easy!'

approach: 1. Write developer contribution guidelines section: prerequisites (Docker, since Go isn't required locally), setup steps, running tests, linting, PR expectations
2. Write Club Adaptation Guide section with subsections:
a. Overview: what jim.tennis does and what you'd get by deploying it
b. Prerequisites: technical skills needed (basic Docker, command line comfort, ideally some Go knowledge for customisation)
c. Configuration changes: environment variables, club name, branding
d. Code changes required: ClubID logic in home/away determination, any hardcoded St Ann's references
e. Known pain points: the apostrophe situation across BHPLTA data, league websites, and internal data — different sources use different characters (', ', ʼ, etc.) and this creates matching issues
f. Deployment: point to existing docs/digitalocean_deployment.md and docs/PRODUCTION_QUICK_REFERENCE.md
g. Getting help: GitHub issues, club enquiry template
3. Review for tone — helpful and honest, not overwhelming or discouraging
key_st_anns_assumptions: internal/admin/ handlers use ClubID to determine home vs away teams
BHPLTA integration assumes St Ann's club code (STANN001)
Various template strings reference St Ann's directly

The apostrophe in St Ann's appears differently across: BHPLTA website, match cards, CSV imports, internal database. This is a known source of bugs and the single biggest barrier to multi-club support.

testing_requirements: All internal links and file references are valid
Setup instructions are accurate and can be followed on a fresh clone
Club adaptation guide file references point to files that actually exist

context.user_story: As a developer or club representative, I want clear guidance on how to contribute code or adapt the system for my club, with honest information about what's involved
